mnemonics.title: SS Components: 'Rows, Columns, Treatments, Error'
mnemonics.content: Remember the four components as 'RCTE' -- 'Real Counting Takes Effort.' SST = SSR (Rows) + SSC (Columns) + SSTr (Treatments) + SSE (Error). Each component has (p-1) degrees of freedom except Error which has (p-1)(p-2).

mnemonics.title: Error df: '(p-1)(p-2) -- One Less, Two Less'
mnemonics.content: The error degrees of freedom in a Latin square is (p-1)(p-2). Think 'One Less times Two Less' than p. For p=4: 3 x 2 = 6. For p=5: 4 x 3 = 12. This formula is derived because total df (p^2 - 1) minus the three sources each with (p-1) df leaves (p-1)(p-2).

mnemonics.title: Latin Square Rule: 'Once Per Row, Once Per Column'
mnemonics.content: The defining rule of a Latin square is like a Sudoku constraint: each treatment letter must appear exactly ONCE in every row and ONCE in every column. Think of it as 'No Repeats in any Row or Column' -- just like the numbers 1-9 in each row and column of Sudoku.
